questions.question: Which of the following factors contributed significantly to the empty treasury of France upon Louis XVI's accession?

options.A: The cost of building the Bastille
options.B: Long years of war and the cost of maintaining the court at Versailles
options.C: High taxes paid by the first two estates
options.D: The abolition of the feudal system

questions.explanation: Long years of war drained financial resources, added to by the cost of maintaining an extravagant court at the Palace of Versailles[cite: 63, 64].

questions.question: How much debt did the French government incur specifically due to its involvement in the American War of Independence?

options.B: 2 billion livres
options.C: More than 1 billion livres
options.D: 10 billion livres

questions.explanation: The war added more than a billion livres to a debt that had already risen to more than 2 billion livres[cite: 66].

questions.question: Which social groups constituted the 'Middle Class' that emerged in the eighteenth century?

options.A: Peasants and artisans who owned land
options.B: Nobles who supported the King's taxes
options.C: Merchants, manufacturers, and professionals like lawyers
options.D: The clergy and royal officials

questions.explanation: The middle class earned wealth through trade and manufacture, and included educated professions such as lawyers[cite: 145, 146].

questions.question: In his 'Two Treatises of Government', John Locke sought to refute which doctrine?

options.A: The doctrine of separation of powers
options.B: The doctrine of the social contract
options.C: The doctrine of the divine and absolute right of the monarch
options.D: The doctrine of universal adult franchise

questions.explanation: Locke sought to refute the idea that monarchs had a divine and absolute right to rule[cite: 150].

questions.question: What led to a 'subsistence crisis' in France during the Old Regime?

options.B: A bad harvest combined with rising population and food prices
options.C: The introduction of the decimal system
options.D: The abolition of the slave trade

questions.explanation: A rapid increase in population led to higher demand for grains; when production couldn't keep pace, prices rose, leading to a subsistence crisis[cite: 122, 123, 128].

questions.question: According to the principle of the Estates General before 1789, how was voting conducted?

options.A: Each individual member had one vote
options.B: Each estate had one vote
options.C: Only the Third Estate could vote
options.D: Voting was done by show of hands in Paris

questions.explanation: Voting in the Estates General had traditionally been conducted according to the principle that each estate had one vote[cite: 198].

questions.question: The members of the Third Estate demanded that voting in the 1789 Estates General be conducted by:

options.A: The first and second estates only
options.B: The assembly as a whole, where each member had one vote
options.C: The King alone
options.D: A committee of five members

questions.explanation: They demanded that each member of the assembly should have one vote, a democratic principle[cite: 200].

questions.question: What was the main purpose of the National Assembly's oath at the indoor tennis court?

options.B: To draft a constitution that would limit the powers of the monarch
options.C: To declare war on Prussia and Austria
options.D: To seize the assets of the Church

questions.explanation: They swore not to disperse until they had drafted a constitution for France to limit the monarch's powers[cite: 205].

questions.question: Which of the following was a result of the decree passed on the night of 4 August 1789?

options.B: The execution of Marie Antoinette
options.C: The abolition of the feudal system of obligations and taxes
options.D: The appointment of Napoleon as Emperor

questions.explanation: The Assembly passed a decree abolishing the feudal system and forced the clergy to give up privileges[cite: 242, 243].

questions.question: In the Political System under the Constitution of 1791, the National Assembly was:

options.B: Indirectly elected by a group of electors
options.C: Appointed by the King
options.D: Composed only of the Clergy

questions.explanation: Citizens voted for a group of electors, who then chose the Assembly[cite: 309, 310].

questions.question: What did the 'Broken Chain' symbolise in revolutionary political imagery?

options.B: Strength lies in unity
options.C: The act of becoming free
options.D: Knowledge driving away ignorance

questions.explanation: Chains were used to fetter slaves; a broken chain stands for the act of becoming free[cite: 377].

questions.question: The Jacobin Club's leader, Maximilian Robespierre, followed a policy of:

options.B: Secret negotiations with the King of Prussia
options.C: Severe control and punishment
options.D: Granting voting rights to women

questions.explanation: Robespierre followed a policy of severe control and punishment during the Reign of Terror[cite: 461].

questions.question: What was the 'pain d’égalité'?

options.B: A loaf made of wholewheat required for all citizens
options.C: Bread given for free to the poor
options.D: A festive bread eaten on 14 July

questions.explanation: To practice equality, all citizens were required to eat this 'equality bread' instead of expensive white flour[cite: 466].

questions.question: Why did the Jacobins wear long striped trousers?

options.A: Because they were part of the army
options.B: To set themselves apart from nobles who wore knee breeches
options.C: Because the King ordered them to
options.D: To show their loyalty to the Church

questions.explanation: They wore long trousers to set themselves apart from fashionable sections of society, especially nobles (the 'knee breeches')[cite: 425, 426].

questions.question: What happened to Louis XVI in January 1793?

options.B: He was executed publicly on the charge of treason
options.C: He was restored to his full powers
options.D: He fled to Austria with his family

questions.explanation: Louis XVI was sentenced to death for treason and executed at the Place de la Concorde[cite: 456, 457].

questions.question: The 'Directory' which ruled France after the fall of the Jacobins consisted of:

options.B: An executive made up of five members
options.C: A committee of 745 members
options.D: The King and his ministers

questions.explanation: The Directory was an executive made up of five members, intended to prevent the concentration of power[cite: 502, 503].

questions.question: Who was Olympe de Gouges?

options.B: A revolutionary woman who wrote a Declaration of Rights for women
options.C: A member of the first estate who supported taxes
options.D: A famous painter who painted the 'Tennis Court Oath'

questions.explanation: She was a politically active woman who wrote the 'Declaration of the Rights of Woman and Citizen'[cite: 552, 555].

questions.question: The 'Triangular Slave Trade' took place between which three regions?

options.B: Europe, Africa, and the Americas
options.C: Britain, France, and Spain
options.D: Paris, Versailles, and Bordeaux

questions.explanation: The trade involved French merchants sailing to Africa for slaves, then to the Caribbean (Americas), then back to Europe[cite: 598, 599].

questions.question: How did Napoleon Bonaparte initially present himself to the people of Europe?

options.A: As a king from the Bourbon family
options.B: As a liberator who would bring freedom
options.C: As a religious leader of the Church
options.D: As a protector of the feudal system

questions.explanation: Initially, many saw Napoleon as a liberator who would bring freedom for the people[cite: 652].

questions.question: Which important law came into effect immediately after the storming of the Bastille in 1789?

options.B: The abolition of slavery
options.C: The abolition of censorship
options.D: The declaration of war on Britain

questions.explanation: One important law that came into effect shortly after the storming of the Bastille was the abolition of censorship[cite: 631].

questions.question: The 'rights of man' were established as 'natural and inalienable' by the Declaration, meaning they:

options.A: Could be taken away by the King at any time
options.B: Belonged to each human being by birth and could not be taken away
options.C: Were only for the members of the National Assembly
options.D: Were only valid during times of war

questions.explanation: These rights belonged to each human being by birth and could not be taken away[cite: 334].

questions.question: What was the significance of the Port cities of Bordeaux and Nantes?

options.A: They were the centers of the Reign of Terror
options.B: They owed their prosperity to the flourishing slave trade
options.C: They were the only cities that refused to pay taxes
options.D: They were the birthplaces of Napoleon

questions.explanation: Port cities like Bordeaux and Nantes prospered due to their involvement in the slave trade[cite: 602].

questions.question: Which of these Indian individuals responded to the ideas coming from revolutionary France?

options.B: Tipu Sultan and Rammohan Roy
options.C: Akbar and Shah Jahan
options.D: Subhash Chandra Bose and Bhagat Singh

questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan and Rammohan Roy are two examples of individuals who responded to these revolutionary ideas[cite: 664].

questions.question: Under the Constitution of 1791, a person's eligibility to be an elector depended on:

options.B: Belonging to the highest bracket of taxpayers
options.C: Being a woman of the third estate
options.D: Serving in the King's personal guard

questions.explanation: To qualify as an elector and then a member of the Assembly, a man had to belong to the highest bracket of taxpayers[cite: 313].

questions.question: The National Assembly declared war against which countries in April 1792?

options.B: Prussia and Austria
options.C: Russia and Italy
options.D: USA and India

questions.explanation: The National Assembly voted in April 1792 to declare war against Prussia and Austria[cite: 408].
